forecast-period.text: A cold front in the Bight will move across the south of SA today followed by a weaker front on Monday. A high pressure system centred near southern WA will begin to dominate the weather pattern across SA mid next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers about the North East Pastoral district contracting eastwards to mostly clear by early afternoon. Showers extending across the agricultural area and southern pastoral districts during the morning and early afternoon. Isolated thunderstorms about the southeast coast and western Kangaroo Island. Possible thunderstorms with showers in the southeast. Maximum temperatures near average, grading to below average about the agricultural area. Freshening north to northwesterly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change extending across the west and agricultural areas during the day, reaching the far northeast during the evening.
Showers about the southern agricultural area, most likely about westward facing coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures mostly below average. West to southwesterly winds, fresh about the coasts.

Showers about the agricultural area, possibly extending into the south of the pastoral districts. Showers more likely about westward facing coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northwest to southwesterly winds, fresh about southern coasts and the southeast. Light winds in the north.

text.p: Isolated showers about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures near to below average. West to southwesterly winds, easing and tending southwest to southeasterly during the day.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Generally less than 2mm across the southern Pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 5 mm across the northern agricultural area and west coast, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the southern agricultural area. Higher totals of 15-30mm are possible about the Mount Lofty Ranges.
